evidence: Across the sequence the meter/readout is the load-bearing element, with the retrieval-prompt card dimmed as a persistent anchor and the two-column table serving as supporting context that pools into the dial.
Elements are introduced progressively: prompt card, then dial, then three table rows, then six rows, then the 'fit 176' readout, never flooding more than four novel items at once.
Column headers 'you' and 'the role' sit atop their number columns and row labels sit beside their values; no boxed legend or corner key maps colors to names.
Text is confined to short labels ('you', 'the role', row names, 'score', 'fit 176') each attached to a visible referent; no spoken-register prose sits alone on the background.
All labels, numbers, and the 'fit 176' readout are rendered at comfortably readable size, well above the ~2.2% frame-height threshold.
The table, dial, and readout all sit fully inside the frame with generous margins; nothing is cropped.
The table columns, dial, and readout are cleanly separated with no overlapping or intersecting text elements.
Observed entries are periwinkle-blue numbers, the dial and 'fit 176' parameter/score readout are lilac, and the arrows are periwinkle, consistent with Night School token semantics.
Every element — prompt card, arrows, dial, table, readout — carries propositional content; no decorative ornaments appear.

The prompt card holds while the meter idles, columns become 'you' and 'the role' with Python hours/SQL reps/nights free plus more rows at readable size, and the entries pool into the lilac dial readout as intended.
Row labels sit beside their values and the dial's readout sits directly beneath the dial that aggregates the matched entries.
The sequence builds continuously: card, then dial with arrows, then three rows, then six rows, then the meter fills and resolves to 'fit 176'.
The six-row table and dial each have ample surrounding negative space and a clear left-to-right reading order into the readout.
